title: Order Processing & Logistics Administrator
description: **Our Story:** We have been farming the fertile lands of North County Dublin for over 200 years. Fast forward to today and our family has grown to include more than just flesh and blood; we’ve got an amazing team at the farm, all of whom we consider as family none\-the\-less. We love working with committed and inspiring people to create something our grandparents would be proud of. From our proud family heritage, hard work and passion for quality, we at Keogh’s Farm are committed to producing Ireland’s finest potatoes, crisps, and snacks that are truly… Grown with Love in Ireland. **Purpose of the Role:** The Order Processing \& Logistics Administrator is a vital part of the supply chain process and is responsible for the order capture, planning, coordinating, administration, monitoring and distribution of Keogh’s Crisps. You will facilitate the smooth running of the department with your excellent customer service skills, as well as the ability to multi\-task and work well under pressure. **Key Areas Of The Role** * Processing, planning, coordinating and monitoring the order process and timely dispatch of goods, with accuracy and efficiency. * Responding efficiently to customer product and sales queries * Processing of invoices, credit notes and debit notes. * Close liaison with the Sales team, Operations Manager, Crisp Production Manager, Warehouse \& Dispatch operatives. Finance Department and Customers regarding deliveries/orders/stock \& other issues. * Planning vehicle routes \& liaising with haulage companies and our own drivers, troubleshooting of any delivery issues that may arise. * Assuring that all correct paperwork is completed and supplied with all transactions and ensuring conformance to specifications. * Using a variety of software packages, including Microsoft Office, Diomac, Retail Exchange, and Customer/Supplier portals * Logging and follow\-through of any non\-conformance issues * Provision of administrative support to the Sales Managers and Sales team. * Ad\-hoc office duties, data entry, reporting, scanning \& filing **Essential Requirements** * Third level qualification desirable. * 2 to 3 years order processing / general administration experience within the FMCG industry. * Strong Customer Service attitude * Excellent interpersonal, written \& verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate confidently at all levels. * Strong computer skills with excellent experience of Microsoft Office. * Self\-starter with the capability to show drive, resilience, and ability to take initiative in a demanding environment. * Diligent individual, taking ownership of all duties of the role from start to completion. * Well organised with excellent attention to detail. * Possess ability to handle pressure and deal with conflicting priorities. * An ability to work effectively as part of a team.

title: PMO Analyst
description: 6 May 2026 Dublin, IE, IE **About Goodbody** Goodbody is a leading financial services firm, committed to serving and growing client relationships for 150 years. We are a full service, investment\-led business offering wealth management, asset management, investment banking and private equity services. Our staff of over 400 work in offices across Dublin, London, Cork and Galway. We’re proud to offer prospective employees a professional, collegial work environment that encourages diversity of thought and background. Our reputation in the Irish market for putting customers first is as consistent as the company’s long\-standing commitment to giving back to the communities that we work in. **About the PMO Analyst Role:** The PMO Analyst provides structured support to the Business Change PMO, helping to ensure that change activity across Goodbody is well governed, accurately reported, and consistently tracked. The role supports existing PMO capability by assisting with governance administration, portfolio reporting, demand tracking, and control activities, enabling the PMO to operate effectively and ensuring decision making forums are supplied with reliable information. **PMO Analyst Accountabilities include:** **Governance, Portfolio \& Change Oversight Support** * Support the coordination and administration of PMO governance forums, including the Change Leadership Committee, Executive Change Council, and Programme/Project Steering Committees. * Assist with the preparation of agendas, papers, and supporting materials. * Capture and maintain meeting minutes, decisions, actions, risks, issues, and dependencies. * Support the maintenance of the enterprise change pipeline, including programme/project registers and tracking of small change, operational, and incident driven requests. * Monitor progress across actions, milestones, and dependencies, highlighting overdue or higher risk items to the PMO Lead where appropriate. **Reporting, Risk \& Data Management** * Collect, consolidate, and maintain data inputs from delivery teams to support: o Status reporting o RAID and dependency tracking o Benefits tracking * Produce draft reports, dashboards, and portfolio views using agreed PMO templates. * Perform basic data quality checks to ensure information is accurate, complete, and timely, flagging gaps or inconsistencies for follow up. * Assist in preparing portfolio, risk, and issue summaries for governance and decision\-making forums. **PMO Standards, Tools \& Knowledge Support** * Support the maintenance and consistent use of PMO governance artefacts, including templates, reporting standards, and core process documentation. * Maintain accurate change and portfolio records in agreed tools (e.g. Azure DevOps), including intake, backlog, and prioritisation views. * Support document version control and structured storage of PMO artefacts. * Assist with title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. This is full\-time temporary role for 2\-3 month. This role has a shift pattern of Monday \- Friday, 1pm\-10pm **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines

title: Part Time Sales Assistant - New Store Opening
description: We’re launching one of our largest Sports Direct stores in Ireland, right in the heart of Henry Street, Dublin and we’re building a high\-performing team to bring it to life. As the UK and Ireland’s No.1 sports retailer, Sports Direct offers an unbeatable range of leading sportswear, footwear, and equipment. With 600\+ stores and growing, we’re part of Frasers Group a bold, ambitious business redefining retail through pace, scale, and innovation. This isn’t just another store opening this is a flagship launch, and we’re looking for people who are ready to deliver from day one. **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate * Flexible working to suit your availability * Staff discount across all Frasers Group brands * Clear progression opportunities within a fast\-growing business * A high\-energy, performance\-driven team environmen **Job Description** As part of our new Henry Street team, you’ll play a key role in delivering an exceptional customer experience and driving commercial performance from opening day. **You Will** * Deliver outstanding customer service across all areas of the store * Provide confident product recommendations and support customer queries * Maintain high standards of visual merchandising and shop floor presentation * Replenish stock efficiently to maximise availability and sales * Process transactions accurately and efficiently * Drive sales through promoting offers and supporting add\-on purchases * Work collaboratively to achieve daily sales and service targets **Qualifications** * Due to the scale and pace of this flagship opening, we’re looking for individuals who can hit the ground running: * Previous retail or customer service experience is essential * Confident communicator with a positive, energetic approach * Commercially focused with a drive to deliver results * Thrives in a fast\-paced, high\-volume environment * Flexible and reliable, especially during peak trading periods * Takes pride in delivering high standards and being part of a winning team **Additional Information** At Sports Direct, we live by our core values: * Think Without Limits – Move fast, take bold action, and bring the team along with you. * Own It and Back Yourself – Take responsibility for your role, master the basics, and drive results. * Be Relevant – Stay connected to our people, our partners, and the planet. Due to the high level of interest expected for this flagship Dublin opening, vacancies may close early. We recommend applying as soon as possible to avoid missing out. Apply now and be part of one of the most exciting retail openings in Dublin.

title: Payroll Specialist
description: **Your new company** This global tech company have been on a growth path over the past years. Having substantially grown in headcount in their Dublin office in recent years, they are seeking to hire a Payroll coordinator for a newly created role, to assist with the processing of payroll and co\-ordination of the external payroll provider. This company are centrally located in Dublin, with other offices across the globe. **Your new role** You will be responsible for ensuring accurate payroll processing and liaising with both internal managers and staff, and external bureau to address any queries or issues relating to payroll. You will oversee all benefit\-in\-kind admin, including health cover and pension admin, and ensure accurate and timely processing. You will work with staff across the Irish business to address queries relating to deductions or other payroll\-related issues. You will also provide in\-depth reports on payroll for both HR and finance, and work closely with your colleagues in Dublin and the UK. **What you'll need to succeed** To be considered, applicants must have : * A minimum of 3\+ years experience working within the Irish payroll \- this is a newly created and key advisory role. Applicants without ample Irish payroll experience will not be the right fit for this particular role. * Previous experience working with health cover admin and pension admin * Knowledge of "my future fund" pension auto\-enrolment an advantage. Desirable : * Previous experience implementing payroll processes, systems or moving a payroll from an external provider to an in\-house processing model. * IPASS qualified, or working towards this. **What you'll get in return** This is a newly created role, offering ample room to grow and develop over the coming years. You will play an integral part in the company and have great room to develop your career. This role offers hybrid working 2\-3 days in office each week. Other benefits include : pension \& health cover. The office is centrally located north of Dublin city and easily accessible from city centre, train and bus routes and parking on site. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

title: Pension Support Associate - 12 month FTC
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** In the role, the successful candidate will: * Develop an in\-depth understanding of Davy's pension product offering including, Master Trusts, Executive Pension Portfolios (EPPs), Personal Retirement Bonds (PRBs), Personal Retirement Savings Account (PRSAs) and Approved Retirement Funds (ARFs) * General pension administration duties including account set up, processing benefits, data input, allocation of premiums and peer reviews; * Ensuring compliance with the relevant pensions rules and legislation governing each of the pension product offerings; * Assisting with the migration of the Davy EPP book of business to alternative Davy arrangements in line with EU Directive, IORPs II * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * 2\- 3 years experience as a Pension Administrator. * Excellent customer service skills; * Team player. * Strong communication skills; * Ability to prioritise work effectively; **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Platform Operations Specialist - Systems Infrastructure and Excellence - T&S
description: Global Operations Platform Operations Specialist \- Systems Infrastructure and Excellence \- T\&S Location : Dublin Employment Type : Regular Job Code : A177218 Responsibilities The Systems Infrastructure and Excellence (SIE) team plays a critical role in enhancing the operational capabilities and infrastructure of the Trust \& Safety (T\&S) organization. We focus on optimizing and automating workflows for T\&S systems to reduce operational costs and drive process excellence through strategic roadmapping and business prioritization. Our core functions include building robust tooling governance that aligns with compliance requirements and removing tooling fragmentation to create a more cohesive environment. We also empower moderation efficiency and quality with AI applications and collaborate with Operations, Engineering and Product teams to deliver user\-centric solutions. Additionally, we build common frameworks to integrate both human and machine moderation and deliver advanced business analytics to provide actionable insights. **Responsibilities:** * User and Business Insight: Deeply understand business requirements by analyzing user feedback, conducting user research, and leveraging data to identify pain points and user needs. * Data Strategy: Define and manage the data index system, identify key metrics, and drive data infrastructure improvements to support business optimization. * Product Implementation: Translate business requirements into clear product documentation (MRD/BRD), develop platform and tool\-based solutions, and coordinate with R\&D teams to ensure successful implementation. * Project Management: Proactively track and manage the progress of platform requirements, from testing and verification to launch and promotion with the business, to ensure timely and high\-quality delivery. * User Enablement: Create and maintain platform operation documents, lead training sessions, and promote new features to boost user adoption and product understanding. * Process Optimization: Continuously identify and improve and automate operational processes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness across the product development lifecycle. * Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Serve as the primary liaison for cross\-functional teams (including R\&D, business, and legal), respond to urgent issues, and coordinate resources to ensure the smooth execution of global projects. Qualifications Minimum Qualifications * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Product Management, or a related technical/analytical field \- within 3 years of graduation * Foundational understanding or experience with AI, LLMs, agents, and AI infrastructure. * Strong ability to quickly understand complex business contexts, operational logic, and system architecture. * Data\-driven mindset with the ability to leverage insights for business optimization. * Exceptional logical thinking, problem\-solving, and business abstraction skills. * Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across product, business, and R\&D teams. **Preferred Qualifications:** * Internship, placement year, early\-career, or professional experience in product, project, operations, or technical program management. * Exposure to technical product development, automation systems, or AI\-driven business processes. * Experience working cross\-functionally in fast\-paced, global environments. * Additional relevant industry experience beyond early\-career stage is considered a plus. Job Information Trust \& Safety Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach. ​ About TikTok TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. ​ Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. ​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. ​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion ​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Pre- Sales Technical Support Engineer
description: **Pre\-Sales Technical Support Engineer** **About Your New Employer** * Join a leading global engineering company known for innovation, continuous learning, and technical excellence. * Benefit from a collaborative technical team, offering guidance and the chance to develop in\-depth knowledge of cutting\-edge HVAC solutions. * Experience a strong focus on training with access to industry\-leading experts and mentoring. **About Your New Job** * As a Pre\-Sales Technical Support Engineer, you will support the external sales team and customers by designing solutions, producing quotations, and providing technical support for heating, cooling, and ventilation equipment. * You will develop expertise in commercial heat pumps, chillers, IT cooling equipment, and AHUs, giving technical guidance on energy efficiency and compliance. * You will interpret technical requests and specifications, generate compliant designs using various software tools, and maintain project enquiries for demand forecasting. * You will collaborate closely with after\-sales teams for commissioning, service visits, and spare parts support. **What Skills You Need** * Third Level Qualification in Engineering, Construction, Design, Sustainability or demonstrated equivalent knowledge. * Excellent communication and organisational skills. * At least 1\+ years’ experience in a related technical support or engineering position (motivated early\-career candidates also considered). * Strong understanding of Microsoft Office Suite; experience with AutoCAD and Zoho platform is a plus. * Positive attitude, ability to learn about new products, and genuine interest in supporting customers. **What's on Offer** * Attractive base salary: €50,000 – €55,000 * Full access to onsite gym * Onsite restaurant (free dinner/lunch) * Annual bonus * Private healthcare * Pension * Career development opportunities in a dynamic and supportive environment.

title: Pre-Sales Solutions Engineer (Italian Speaking)
description: *At Klaviyo, we value the unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives each Klaviyo (we call ourselves Klaviyos) brings to our workplace each and every day. We believe everyone deserves a fair shot at success and appreciate the experiences each person brings beyond the traditional job requirements. If you're a close but not exact match with the description, we hope you'll still consider applying. Want to learn more about life at Klaviyo? Visit* *klaviyo.com/careers* *to see how we empower creators to own their own destiny.* **Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer** ================================= *Commercial \| EMEA \| Italian Speaking* **About the Opportunity** ========================= Solutions Engineers at Klaviyo are technical sellers at the heart of our revenue motion. We don't just demo software. We work as trusted advisors, helping fast\-growing commercial businesses understand how Klaviyo can solve their real challenges, and then we prove it. We're looking for a Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer to join our EMEA Commercial team, covering the Entrepreneur and SMB segment. This is a great opportunity for someone early in their pre\-sales career who is curious, eager to learn, and excited by the intersection of technology and sales. You don't need to have all the answers on day one. What matters is that you ask great questions, pick things up quickly, and genuinely want to help customers succeed. You'll work alongside Account Executives across the Italian\-speaking market and wider EMEA region, supporting the technical side of deals and building your skills in a collaborative, high\-energy team environment. **What We Do as Solutions Engineers** ===================================== We're technical sellers who drive revenue by winning Commercial deals. Our job is to own the technical win. That means building champions, demonstrating clear value, and making sure prospects choose Klaviyo. We work directly with customers to design solutions that integrate into their existing tech stacks, taking a consultative approach that builds confidence and gets buy\-in from both technical and business stakeholders. Our team builds beyond the day\-to\-day too. Recent projects include AI\-powered demo data generators, a Chrome extension for troubleshooting API calls, and templatised mobile apps to showcase our SDK capabilities. We're a team that builds things to solve problems, and we'd love you to grow into that. **How You'll Make a Difference** ================================ **Support the Technical Win** ----------------------------- * Work alongside Account Executives to support the technical side of Commercial opportunities from qualification through to close. * Participate in technical discovery conversations, learning how to uncover a prospect's goals, current tools, and key constraints. * Deliver product demos and presentations to business and technical audiences, connecting Klaviyo's capabilities to what the customer actually cares about. * Help scope proofs of concept and pilot projects, supporting delivery and keeping stakeholders informed along the way. * Learn how to handle common objections and navigate competitive questions with guidance from the broader team. **Build Customer Confidence** ----------------------------- * Develop a strong understanding of Klaviyo's platform, APIs, and integrations so you can answer technical questions clearly and confidently. * Build relationships with prospects and support them on topics like data strategy, integrations, and implementation approach. * Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Support on RFPs, security reviews, and technical evaluations where needed. * Start building knowledge of the competitive landscape so you can add value in deal conversations over time. **Contribute to the Team** -------------------------- * Help build and improve repeatable assets like demo narratives, templates, and playbooks that make the wider team more effective. * Use AI tools to support your work, from research and demo prep to light automation. * Share what you're hearing from customers back to Product and GTM teams, especially around gaps and trends in the SMB segment. **Who You Are** =============== **Required** ------------ * Fluent in Italian and English, written and spoken. * 1\+ years of experience in a pre\-sales, solutions engineering, technical sales, or customer\-facing technical role at a B2B SaaS company. * Curiosity about technology and a genuine interest in how businesses use data and marketing tools to grow. * Comfortable talking to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders and adapting your communication style accordingly. * Good at explaining things clearly, whether that's a technical concept to a marketer or a business problem to a developer. * Willing to run live product demos and get comfortable thinking on your feet when questions come up. * A proactive mindset and willingness to learn quickly in a fast\-moving environment. **Nice to Have** ---------------- * Some familiarity with web technologies, REST APIs, or data integrations, even if you haven't worked with them professionally. * Exposure to marketing technology, eCommerce platforms (e.g. Shopify, Magento), or customer engagement tools. * Basic experience with JavaScript, Python, or SQL for exploring data or light integration work. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor) and an interest in using them to work smarter. * Any experience in a commercial or SMB\-focused environment, whether in sales, support, or a technical capacity. * Additional spoken European languages **What Sets You Apart** ----------------------- * You ask great questions and genuinely listen to the answers. * You're not afraid to say you don't know something, but you always follow up with the answer. * You pick up new tools and concepts quickly and enjoy figuring things out. * You take ownership of your work and follow through without needing to be chased. * You're excited about the idea of building a career in pre\-sales and see this role as a serious starting point. **How We'll Measure Success** ============================= * Contribution to technical win rate in the Commercial segment. * Quality of demos, discovery conversations, and handoffs to Customer Success and implementation teams. * Speed and consistency in picking up product knowledge and ramping into deals. * Contributions to team assets and the feedback you bring back from customer conversations. \#LI\-Hybrid We use Covey as part of our hiring and / or promotional process. For jobs or candidates in NYC, certain features may qualify it as an AEDT. As part of the evaluation process we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate submitted applications. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound on April 3, 2025\. Please see the independent bias audit report covering our use of Covey here *This role may require up to 10% travel for purposes such as new hire onboarding, client or partner work if applicable, team meetings, and industry events. Travel is coordinated in advance.* **Get to Know Klaviyo** We're Klaviyo (pronounced clay\-vee\-oh). We empower creators to own their destiny by making first\-party data accessible and actionable like never before. We see limitless potential for the technology we're developing to nurture personalized experiences in ecommerce and beyond. To reach our goals, we need our own crew of remarkable creators—ambitious and collaborative teammates who stay focused on our north star: delighting our customers. If you're ready to do the best work of your career, where you'll be welcomed as your whole self from day one and supported with generous benefits, we hope you'll join us. *AI fluency at Klaviyo includes responsible use of AI (including privacy, security, bias awareness, and human\-in\-the\-loop). We provide accommodations as needed.* *By participating in Klaviyo's interview process, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and will adhere to our* *Guidelines for using AI in the Klaviyo interview Process**. For more information about how we process your personal data, see our* *Job Applicant Privacy Notice**.* *Klaviyo is committed to a policy of equal opportunity and non\-discrimination. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, citizenship, national origin, color, religion or religious creed, age, sex (including pregnancy), gender identity, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, veteran or active military status, marital status, criminal record, genetics, retaliation, sexual harassment or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.* *IMPORTANT NOTICE: Our company takes the security and privacy of job applicants very seriously. We will never ask for payment, bank details, or personal financial information as part of the application process. All our legitimate job postings can be found on our official career site. Please be cautious of job offers that come from non\-company email addresses (@klaviyo.com), instant messaging platforms, or unsolicited calls.* **By clicking "Submit Application" you consent to Klaviyo processing your Personal Data in accordance with our Job Applicant Privacy Notice. If you do not wish for Klaviyo to process your Personal Data, please do not submit an application.***You can find our Job Applicant Privacy Notice* *here* *and* *here* *(FR).*

title: Product Graduate
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** ------------------- The POS \& Gateway Face to Face team is seeking a graduate to support key activities across the in‑person payments ecosystem, including payment terminals, gateways, and partner integrations. This role is designed as a rotational graduate program, providing exposure across multiple areas of the department. The graduate will work closely with Product, Technology, Operations, and Vendor partners to support testing, analysis, and operational delivery of POS solutions. The role offers hands‑on experience in a regulated, enterprise payments environment and is ideal for a candidate interested in fintech, payments, technology, or product roles. Our 2\-year graduate program (undergraduate) will give you the opportunity to learn all aspects of the business and you will complete three rotations of 8 months: **Rotation 1** **UAT and QA Testing** * Support user acceptance and quality assurance testing activities across POS and gateway solutions. **Rotation 2** **Production Support** * Assist with operational support for live POS and gateway environments. **Rotation 3 Terminal Management Systems (TMS)** * Gain exposure to terminal lifecycle management and configuration systems. From day one, you'll make meaningful contributions to our business while developing your business acumen, sharpening your skills and growing your network. As a graduate you'll receive: * Intentional, on\-the\-job learning supplemented with formal professional development * Immersive experiences to build skills that are in demand today and in the future * Transitional support catered to your unique needs as a student * A cohort and network of peers, advisors and senior leader advocates * Social and volunteer events that build meaningful connection **What the Graduate Will Gain** * End‑to‑end exposure to POS and payment gateway ecosystems * Practical experience in testing, production support, and product delivery * Understanding of enterprise payments, terminals, and vendor integrations * Experience working in a regulated, global payments environment * Mentorship from experienced professionals across Product, Technology, and Operations **Who we’re looking for** We’re looking for intellectually curious and creative thinkers who can think on their feet and thoughtfully react to a changing market. **Core Bahaviours** * Collaboration – Build partnerships and promote a culture of teamwork. * Customer Centricity – Build strong customer relationships and deliver customer centric solutions. * Drive for Results – Hold self and others accountable to consistently achieve meaningful results. * Ethics \& Trust – Maintain high ethical standards; gain the confidence and trust of others through honesty, integrity and authenticity. * Inclusion – Build a culture of equity and inclusion in which diverse ideas, talent and perspectives are welcomed and encouraged. * Risk \- Appropriately identify and manage risks, in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, and with Company Policy. **Basic qualifications:** * Pursuing a any Business Bachelor’s degree in with a target completion date of May 2026 or class of 2025 * 1 year or less industry experience. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills * Attention to detail and a structured approach to work * Good written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work across technical and non‑technical stakeholders * Willingness to learn and adapt in a fast‑paced environment * Ability to start graduate program on 1 Sept 2026 **Preferred qualifications:** * Interest in payments, fintech, or enterprise technology * Basic understanding of software testing, APIs, or IT systems * Experience using tools such as Jira, Confluence, or similar (not mandatory) **Location:** Arklow. * The role offers a hybrid / flexible schedule, which means there's an in\-office expectation of 3 or more days per week and the flexibility to work outside the office location for the other days. **Contract Type:** * 2 year Fixed term Contract This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * **Clearly defined salary ranges** aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * **Performance\-based incentives** for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * **Inclusive equitable benefits** that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * **Continuous development opportunities** including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * **Employee recognition programs** that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Product Graduate
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. title: Product Owner
description: **About The Role** As the Product Owner for Operations Excellence you will lead the design, delivery and continuous enhancement of Operations Products across the full value chain of Allianz Partners Health globally. This is a highly visible role that will lead a dedicated product squad to deliver customer focused solutions in support of our business strategy. You will collaborate closely with senior Operations and business leaders, internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure initiatives are well designed and delivered at scale and speed. The successful candidate will require strong Operations understanding with product ownership experience in a regional or global setting. Core deliverables will be focused on business centric solutions that are AI embedded by default and scaled globally. What you will do Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to, the following: **Product Ownership \& Delivery** * Own and manage the design, development and continuous improvement of AI driven product solutions across Health Operations globally. * Lead a squad of technical experts including engineers and developers to ensure delivery in an agile methodology at speed. * Translate business requirements into detailed stories, acceptance criteria, rules and prioritised backlogs. * Partner with internal stakeholders and external vendors to execute discovery testing and rollout activities. * Ensure digital solutions meet operational objectives, integrate effectively with existing platforms and scale across regions. * Maintain a clear roadmap of enhancements based on performance metrics, operations feedback, compliance requirements and user insights. * Act as a champion for AI driven transformation by demonstrating clear business value and adoption outcomes. * Build both an internal and external network to ensure that there is early awareness of both new and emerging technologies with business application **Process Transformation** * Partner closely with portfolio management and the business leadership to understand business objectives, validate opportunities and align priorities. * Lead workshops and design sessions to define solution scope, rules and decision logic. * Drive operational readiness, including user training, documentation and change management. * Support governance activities to ensure automation rules and decisions remain compliant, auditable and well controlled. **Stakeholder Engagement** * Act as primary liaison between the Operations functional teams, Operations Support teams, IT and vendor delivery teams. * Communicate progress, risks and outcomes to senior stakeholders through structured updates and dashboards. * Collaborate with Data Analytics teams, Operations Support teams and Regional teams to ensure solutions provide appropriate reporting functionality and meet relevant compliance requirements. **AI Governance, Compliance \& Risk Management** * Ensure solutions comply with group standards, global regulatory requirements and emerging obligations. * Identify potential compliance gaps in how AI tools and rules are used ensuring remediation actions where needed. * Work closely with Compliance, Legal and Risk to ensure proper documentation, transparency and monitoring. **Performance \& Continuous Improvement** * Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) such as cycle\-time reduction, manual touchpoint elimination and accuracy improvement. * Monitor platform performance and user adoption to drive continuous iteration and enhancement. * Contribute to the development of best practices and reuseable frameworks for future initiatives across Operations. **What You Will Bring** To be successful in this position you will need to have the following skills / experience: **Essential** * Degree or relevant professional qualifications in IT, Business Operations or other technical disciplines. * Minimum of 10 years experience in operational improvement, business improvement, digital transformation or automation of which a significant portion of this time should have been spent leading product ownership. * Experience in regulated process industries where there has been a requirement to deliver AI enabled tools and automation solutions. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills to identify areas for improvement and implement effective solutions. * Strong understanding of AI technologies, machine learning, and data analytics to effectively manage and improve the AI product. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skill, with experience engaging senior business and technical audiences. * High attention to detail and ability to deliver to deadlines in a fast paced agile environment. **Desirable** * Understanding of healthcare industry standards, regulations and operational practices although other relevant experience will be considered. **Personal Attributes** * Results driven and pragmatic, focused on achieving measurable outcomes. * Able to balance strategic thinking with hands on delivery. * Confident communicator and facilitator, able to build trust across diverse stakeholder groups. * Collaborative and resilient, thriving in a dynamic and global environment. How we hire *To Recruitment Agencies* *Allianz recruits through dedicated in\-house teams and does not accept unsolicited resumes/CVs, candidate introductions, or outreach from agencies or search firms. We only engage agencies under a prior, written contract and, where applicable, via our Preferred Supplier List (PSL). No fees will be paid for any candidate submitted or introduced without a contract in place, even if that candidate is subsequently hired. Do not contact hiring managers directly. Any breach, including unsolicited submissions, off\-PSL contact, or attempts to invoice without an agreement, will result in candidate disqualification, termination of any existing contract, and non\-payment of fees.* 97837 \| Product Management \| Professional \| Non\-Executive \| Allianz Partners \| Full\-Time \| Permanent Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. Caring for our employees, their ambitions, dreams and challenges, is what makes us a unique employer. Together we can build an environment where everyone feels empowered and has the confidence to explore, to grow and to shape a better future for our customers and the world around us. At Allianz, we stand for unity: we believe that a united world is a more prosperous world, and we are dedicated to consistently advocating for equal opportunities for all. And the foundation for this is our inclusive workplace, where people and performance both matter, and nurtures a culture grounded in integrity, fairness, inclusion and trust. We therefore welcome applications regardless of ethnicity or cultural background, age, gender, nationality, religion, social class, disability or sexual orientation, or any other characteristics protected under applicable local laws and regulations. **Join us.** Let's care for tomorrow.

title: Product Support, Bridge
description: Who we are **About Bridge** We’re creating an entirely new payments platform, built with stablecoins, to simplify global money movement. Bridge enables faster, cheaper payments and borderless access to dollars via stablecoins. Through our APIs, businesses can send and receive funds across borders faster / cheaper vs. SWIFT and other fiat\-only rails. Our virtual accounts enable international consumers and businesses to easily access, store and spend US dollars. Our payouts infrastructure enables platforms to disburse USD to anyone globally. We believe many trillions of dollars will move and settle through stablecoin payment rails. Bridge is pulling this future forward. We have a small team of people who have previously built financial infrastructure at some of the world’s leading companies (Coinbase, Stripe, Square, Brex, Upstart, DoorDash, Airbnb) and each and every one of them chose Bridge because they fundamentally believe that stablecoins will be a critical piece of financial infrastructure that allows for the improvement of global money movement. What you’ll do As part of our growing Product Support team, you will be a critical driver in delivering exceptional user experiences. This role goes beyond basic troubleshooting—it's about using your technical expertise, analytical mindset, and project management skills to strategically improve our support systems, processes, and product quality. You will own complex technical user issues and coordinate with cross functional teams, including Engineering and Product, to drive them to resolution. A deep understanding of SQL and APIs will allow you to engage with these teams effectively, analyze data to inform decisions, and propose solutions that drive operational efficiency. Your work will focus on scaling support operations, creating better user experiences, and pushing forward continuous improvements. **Responsibilities** * Analyze and troubleshoot complex technical issues through direct user interaction, utilizing SQL to extract and interpret data, and leveraging your knowledge of API documentation. * Develop product and platform expertise, working closely with Engineering, Product, and Operations teams to diagnose and resolve user issues. * Strategically optimize and scale support processes to enhance efficiency and improve the overall user experience, incorporating data\-driven insights and metrics. * Lead continuous improvement initiatives aimed at hitting key performance metrics such as Consumer Satisfaction (CSAT), Contact Rate, and SLA compliance. * Create and refine documentation to empower users to resolve issues via self\-service, reducing dependency on support teams. * Collaborate and advocate with Product and Engineering to proactively improve the platform based on user feedback, ensuring long\-term product reliability. * Lead cross\-functional projects aimed at identifying inefficiencies in current processes and drive the implementation of long\-term solutions. * Constantly challenge the status quo and push for innovation in user support strategies and operational processes. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * 5\+ years of experience in a customer\-facing product support role, with a focus on troubleshooting technical issues. * Proficiency in SQL for data analysis and querying, with the ability to interpret datasets. * Experience working with API’s. * Exceptional problem\-solving skills, capable of diagnosing complex issues and driving them to resolution. * Proven experience in stakeholder management, with the ability to influence cross\-functional teams and drive progress. * Experience in project management, particularly in optimizing processes, workflows, or support operations. **Preferred Qualifications** * Strong analytical skills with a passion for interpreting data to improve decision\-making and outcomes. * Comfortable explaining technical concepts to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Proficiency in technical environments and the ability to diagnose and resolve highly technical user issues. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual US base salary range for this role is $124,000 \- $186,000\. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings ("OTE") range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the US may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Additional benefits for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; 401(k) plan; medical, dental, and vision benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Programme Quality Liaison
description: *Permanent Position* **Purpose of this Role** The Programme Quality Liaison Officer supports the development, implementation, and continuous enhancement of quality assurance systems across academic provision and services. The role is responsible for supporting the maintenance of quality and award standards across credit\-bearing and non\-credit\-bearing programmes, including the implementation of national and European quality assurance guidelines.The postholder will plan, organise, and manage annual and periodic quality review processes, including programme monitoring, validation, and revalidation activities, and will support engagement with external stakeholders in relation to institutional evaluation and programme approval. The role requires a high level of rigour, strong project management capability, effective data management and analysis, and the ability to build strong collaborative relationships across academic and professional functions.**About Us** Leadership. It's more than a job, it's a mindset. It's the capacity to inspire, empower and create new possibilities. IMI's mission, and passion, is to equip leaders to build the future. We are world\-class specialists in enabling and inspiring leadership performance. We challenge and support leaders to fulfil their ambition, unlocking their potential and giving them the skills to deliver exceptional performance. We've been shaping world\-class executives for over 75 years. Founded by business leaders, for business leaders, we deliver intensively practical executive education that works in the real world. Our global rankings are a testament to our impact. Any day spent at IMI challenges assumptions, deepens perspective and amplifies ability to lead. Do you have what it takes to work with IMI? Join us on our mission to equip leaders to build the future, and you will shape the future of leadership in Ireland. IMI. Inspiring Leadership Performance. **Work Location and Hybrid Working** This role is based at IMI's headquarters in Dublin. IMI recognises the importance of flexible work arrangements and promotes a hybrid work model that combines in\-person and virtual work. Staff/Faculty have the flexibility to work remotely for a part of their working hours, subject to operational requirements and the agreement of their line manager as outlined in the Work Life Balance Policy. The postholder should demonstrate flexibility in terms of extended and out of normal working hours, on and off campus, prior to and during quality reviews. Travel within Ireland and, occasionally, internationally may be required to attend meetings, conferences, and client engagements, including national and international conferences with quality assurance and enhancement themes within the higher education sector, and representation of IMI on awarding body or other committees and working groups. **Key Responsibilities** This job description outlines the primary duties and requirements of the position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all duties and responsibilities. IMI reserves the right to amend and change the job description as needed. 1\.Quality Assurance Systems and Policy Support the design, implementation, and continuous improvement of quality assurance policies, procedures, and systems Maintain and update the quality framework and associated documentation with accuracy and consistency Ensure alignment with national and European quality assurance guidelines and standards Contribute to the development and documentation of procedures and operational models to support quality assurance activities 2\. Programme Monitoring and Periodic Review* Work closely with the Head of Learning Development \& Quality to plan and implement the quality review cycle for credit\-bearing and non\-credit\-bearing programmes and services * **Support the development and implementation of fit\-for\-purpose processes and criteria for:** * ongoing programme monitoring annual review * periodic programme review * validation and revalidation * Develop and maintain systems, templates, and record\-keeping processes to support programme review activities * **Organise and manage review schedules, including:** * coordinating review timelines * sourcing and liaising with appropriate reviewers * supporting the appointment of review panels and external examiners * **Support and guide programme teams in:** * the preparation for programme review * the implementation of quality improvement plans arising from review outcomes * Seek and incorporate feedback from stakeholders to enhance the effectiveness of review processes 3\. Academic Governance and Reporting* Provide operational support to academic governance structures (e.g. Academic Council, Exam Boards, Quality and Enhancement of Learning Committee, and other bodies) * Ensure accurate documentation, record\-keeping, and tracking of actions * Manage and communicate programme and participant data to governance bodies * **Coordinate the collection and reporting of:** * external examiner reports * programme review outcomes * programme team responses and action plans * Prepare high\-quality, evidence\-based reports for internal committees and external bodies, including professional, regulatory, statutory, or ranking organisations 4\. External Engagement and Quality Reviews* **Support engagement with external stakeholders in relation to:** * institutional evaluation * programme validation and revalidation * Assist in the coordination and project management of external reviews and audits * Ensure that documentation and evidence are systematically prepared to demonstrate compliance with awarding and quality assurance expectations * Build and maintain effective professional relationships with external stakeholders 5\. Assessment and Standards* Support processes relating to assessment, moderation, and results approval * Assist the Registry and Records Manager in the administration and tracking of recheck, review, and appeal processes * Ensure the integrity, accuracy, and consistency of assessment\-related data and processes * Support the maintenance of award standards across programmes 6\. Data Management and Quality Enhancement* Coordinate the collection, validation, and management of quality\-related data (e.g. learner feedback, progression, completion rates) * Analyse data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement * Support the preparation of self\-evaluation and quality enhancement reports * Work with relevant colleagues to ensure availability and accuracy of data to support programme monitoring and review 7\. Quality and Learning Enhancement Project Management* Support and lead quality assurance and enhancement projects as required * Develop and manage project plans, timelines, and deliverables * Coordinate contributions from multiple stakeholders * Monitor progress, identify risks, and take corrective action where necessary * Propose solutions and initiatives to address issues arising in quality processes 8\. Collaboration and Relationship Management The postholder will: Work collaboratively with colleagues and associate faculty to ensure that programme monitoring and review processes are implemented effectively, consistently, and in a timely manner Liaise and communicate effectively with programme directors to ensure delivery of review schedules and resolution of issues Work closely with colleagues across functions (including registry and other support services) to support reporting and data provision Build strong, constructive working relationships to support a culture of quality enhancement 9\. Training and Support* Provide guidance and support to staff on quality assurance processes and requirements * Support programme teams in understanding and implementing quality processes * Contribute to the development of guidance materials and training initiatives * Promote a culture of quality, consistency, and continuous improvement 10\. General Responsibilities* Provide reports and updates to the Head of Learning Development \& Quality as required * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the organisation * Undertake other duties as required, including providing cover for colleagues where necessary **Candidate Requirements** * A Level 8 qualification in a relevant discipline. * A minimum of three years' relevant experience working within a higher education institution, ideally within a quality or programme/course management setting. * Additional academic or professional qualifications in quality management, business improvement, organisational development or change management would be an advantage. * Strong understanding of academic quality assurance processes, awarding body requirements and programme review cycles. * Excellent organisational and planning skills, with the ability to manage multiple review processes simultaneously and deliver to deadlines. * Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to produce clear reports and procedural documentation. * Proven ability to build effective working relationships with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders. * High level of discretion and the ability to handle sensitive and confidential matters with professionalism. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and relevant data management or reporting systems. **Recruitment Process** To ensure IMI recruits the right people, we take a competency\-based approach to interviewing. Candidates called to interview will be asked a series of competency questions to allow them to demonstrate how their skills and attitudes align with IMI's values and culture. The selection process for this role is conducted in accordance with IMI's Recruitment and Selection Policy, a copy of which is available from hr@imi.ie **Equal Opportunities** IMI is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and respectful workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of age, gender, race, ethnicity, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law. We are dedicated to ensuring that our recruitment processes are fair, transparent, and accessible to all. If you require any reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please do not hesitate to contact us at hr@imi.ie IMI is committed to protecting your personal information and we process and protect your personal data in accordance with our Privacy Notice, which can be accessed on our website at: www.imi.ie/about\-imi/careers\-imi

Collaboration and Relationship Management The postholder will: Work collaboratively with colleagues and associate faculty to ensure that programme monitoring and review processes are implemented effectively, consistently, and in a timely manner Liaise and communicate effectively with programme directors to ensure delivery of review schedules and resolution of issues Work closely with colleagues across functions (including registry and other support services) to support reporting and data provision Build strong, constructive working relationships to support a culture of quality enhancement 9\. Training and Support * Provide guidance and support to staff on quality assurance processes and requirements * Support programme teams in understanding and implementing quality processes * Contribute to the development of guidance materials and training initiatives * Promote a culture of quality, consistency, and continuous improvement 10\. General Responsibilities * Provide reports and updates to the Head of Learning Development \& Quality as required * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the organisation * Undertake other duties as required, including providing cover for colleagues where necessary **Candidate Requirements** * A Level 8 qualification in a relevant discipline. * A minimum of three years' relevant experience working within a higher education institution, ideally within a quality or programme/course management setting. * Additional academic or professional qualifications in quality management, business improvement, organisational development or change management would be an advantage. * Strong understanding of academic quality assurance processes, awarding body requirements and programme review cycles. * Excellent organisational and planning skills, with the ability to manage multiple review processes simultaneously and deliver to deadlines. * Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to produce clear reports and procedural documentation. * Proven ability to build effective working relationships with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders. * High level of discretion and the ability to handle sensitive and confidential matters with professionalism. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and relevant data management or reporting systems. **Recruitment Process** To ensure IMI recruits the right people, we take a competency\-based approach to interviewing.
